channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Delta revenue hits ₦200bn, RMAFC hails diversification drive
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Delta State’s Internally Generated Revenue (IGR) soars to ₦200bn, a 138% increase, as RMAFC commends its economic diversification efforts.
Read More: https://punchng.com/delta-revenue-hits-%e2%82%a6200bn-rmafc-hails-diversification-drive/

item.title: Bauchi CP orders probe claims of torture, killing of 20-year-old man by officers
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Bauchi State Police Command investigates claims of torture and death of a 20-year-old man by officers. Justice promised in Bauchi police brutality case.
Read More: https://punchng.com/bauchi-cp-orders-probe-claims-of-torture-killing-of-20-year-old-man-by-officers/

item.title: BREAKING: House withdraws own state police bill, considers Tinubu’s proposal
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The House of Representatives withdraws its state police bill to consider President Tinubu’s proposal, marking a significant shift in the legislative proces
Read More: https://punchng.com/breaking-house-withdraws-own-state-police-bill-considers-tinubus-proposal/

item.title: Turn off addictive features on social media for children, say EU lawmakers
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
EU lawmakers are pushing for a “youth mode” on social media platforms to turn off addictive features and targeted advertising for children, ensuring their
Read More: https://punchng.com/turn-off-addictive-features-on-social-media-for-children-say-eu-lawmakers/

item.title: Atiku demands probe of Gbajabiamila over fresh corruption allegations
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Atiku Abubakar calls for an independent probe into fresh corruption allegations against President Tinubu’s Chief of Staff, Femi Gbajabiamila.
Read More: https://punchng.com/atiku-demands-probe-of-gbajabiamila-over-fresh-corruption-allegations/

item.title: New UNIPORT VC warns against cultism, sexual harassment
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The new UNIPORT VC, Prof. Princewill Chike, issues a stern warning against cultism, sexual harassment, and other vices, vowing to uphold discipline.
Read More: https://punchng.com/new-uniport-vc-warns-against-cultism-sexual-harassment/

item.title: NYSC to blacklist employers denying corps members accommodation, transport
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
NYSC will blacklist employers failing to provide accommodation and transport for corps members. DG urges improved welfare for enhanced productivity.
Read More: https://punchng.com/nysc-to-blacklist-employers-denying-corps-members-accommodation-transport/

item.title: ADC seeks Nafiu Gombe’s prosecution over forged INEC portal claim
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The African Democratic Congress demands Nafiu Gombe’s prosecution after INEC debunked his false claims of uploading candidates for the 2027 elections.
Read More: https://punchng.com/adc-seeks-nafiu-gombes-prosecution-over-forged-inec-portal-claim/

item.title: ITF screens 225,000 artisans nationwide for skills acquisition
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Industrial Training Fund (ITF) is screening 225,000 artisans nationwide for its Skill Up Artisans (SUPA) programme, offering globally competitive skill
Read More: https://punchng.com/itf-screens-225000-artisans-nationwide-for-skills-acquisition/

item.title: You don't realise how steep Eau Rouge really is!
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: See how steep Belgium's Eau Rouge climb really is from this dramatic camera angle.

item.title: Police raid ciminal hideouts in Abia, arrest 31 over drugs
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Abia State Police Command raids criminal hideouts, arresting 31 suspects for illicit drug possession. Learn more about the ongoing operations.
Read More: https://punchng.com/police-raid-ciminal-hideouts-in-abia-arrest-31-over-drugs/

item.title: Man City interested in Morocco wonderkid Bouaddi
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Manchester City are showing interest in Lille midfielder Ayyoub Bouaddi.

item.title: Breaking: House of Reps withdraws own State Police Bill
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The House of Representatives has withdrawn its proposed constitutional amendment seeking to establish state police in Nigeria.

item.title: Spain Target First-Ever World Cup Win Against France
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Spain and France will renew their rivalry when both heavyweights clash today (Tuesday) in the semifinals of the FIFA World Cup 2026. To get to this stage of the tournament France were flawless in the group stage as they topped with nine points before seeing off Sweden (round of 32) Paraguay (round o

item.title: How new Yemen tensions could complicate the global energy crisis
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: If the fighting in Yemen expands, and the Houthis try to block the Bab al-Mandeb, the world economy faces a new shock.

item.title: DMO reopens three FGN bonds, targets N1.2 trillion at July auction
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The Debt Management Office (DMO), on behalf of the Federal Government of Nigeria, has announced plans to raise up to N1.2 trillion through the reopening of three Federal Government of Nigeria (FGN) bonds. The post DMO reopens three FGN bonds, targets N1.2 trillion at July auction appeared first on N

item.title: 🔴 WHO estimates DR Congo Ebola outbreak could be up to four times higher than official toll
item.dc:creator: France 24

item.title: A’Ibom: Over 7000 Vulnerable Households Received Free Food Items In Urue Offong/Oruko
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Relief came to vulnerable households in Urue Offong/Oruko Local Government Area of Akwa Ibom state as the State Government commenced the distribution of food items under the fourth phase of the ARISE Free Food Distribution Programme, reaffirming its commitment to supporting citizens amid prevailing

item.title: Reps demand details of CBN, NNPCL’s unremitted operating surplus
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The House of Representatives Public Accounts Committee (PAC) has directed the Office of the Accountant-General of the Federation (OAGF) to provide a comprehensive breakdown of outstanding operating surplus and other revenues allegedly owed to the Federal Government by the Central Bank of Nigeria (CB

item.title: ‘Prison worse than being broke’ – Reactions trail video of DJ Chicken in Kirikiri
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: A video showing controversial content creator and disc jockey, DJ Chicken, at the Kirikiri prison has sparked reactions from Nigerians. DAILY POST reports that the entertainer was remanded in Kirikiri prison after he allegedly made threatening remarks against President Bola Tinubu’s son, Seyi.

item.title: Court Jails Man 20 Years For Concealing Information About Notorious Bandit
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: The federal high court in Abuja has sentenced one Muhammad Sani to 20 years imprisonment for withholding information about the activities of an alleged notorious bandit. In January 2023, Sani was arrested by operatives of the Department of State Services (DSS). He was arraigned on a four-count charg

item.title: JPMorgan Sees Record Profit as Stock-Trading Climbs 86%
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: JPMorgan Chase reported its highest quarterly profit ever as the second-quarter equities haul climbed 86% from a year earlier to $6.03 billion. Gerard Cassidy, head of US bank strategy and large-cap bank analyst at RBC examines the results on “Bloomberg Surveillance.” (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Tested By Water, Trusted By Firefighters: The itel Power 80 Story
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Smartphone durability is often communicated through specification sheets and laboratory certifications. Water resistance ratings, drop tests and impact claims have become common across the industry. But for consumers, the real question is simpler: can a phone continue working when conditions are far

item.title: Yobe, Adamawa tighten security to curb illegal mining
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The measures may not be unconnected with the allegations associating solid minerals, such as gold, with banditry and insurgency in some parts of the country The post Yobe, Adamawa tighten security to curb illegal mining appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: FIFA Appoints American Referee Ismail Elfath For England vs Argentina World Cup Semi-Final
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: FIFA has appointed American referee Ismail Elfath to officiate Wednesday’s 2026 FIFA World Cup semi-final between England and Argentina. The world football governing body announced the appointment in a post on its official X account on Tuesday, confirming that Elfath will oversee Match 102 at Atlant

item.title: Bank of America stock falls despite earnings beat
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Shares in Bank of America declined in pre-market trading after the banking giant posted a 15% jump in revenue year-on-year.

item.title: I Fled South Africa Without My 2 Daughters After 3 Xenophobic Attacks — Returnee
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A Nigerian returnee, Okafor Ugochukwu, has recounted how repeated xenophobic attacks in South Africa forced him to flee the country without his two daughters after losing his businesses, home and means of livelihood. Speaking in an interview with Vanguard on Tuesday, Ugochukwu, an indigene of Anambr

item.title: 2027 Ogun Guber Race: Senator Adeola (Yayi) Commissions 4-Storey Ultra-Modern Campaign Office
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The All Progressives Congress (APC) governorship candidate for the 2027 Ogun State election, Senator Solomon Olamilekan Adeola, popularly known as Yayi, has commissioned his ultra-modern four-storey Governorship Campaign Headquarters on Sunday, July 12, 2026, in Ibara, Abeokuta, Ogun State. The four

item.title: BREAKING: House of Reps withdraws own State Police Bill [VIDEO]
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The House of Representatives on Tuesday withdrew its own proposed constitutional amendment seeking to establish state police in the country. This came after lawmakers moved ahead with consideration of a separate State Police Bill forwarded by the Executive arm of government. At Tuesda

item.title: Customs Intercepts N26.56bn Cannabis Concealed With Vehicles At Apapa Port
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Nigeria Customs Service (NCS), Apapa Area Command, has seized Cannabis Indica valued at ₦26.56 billion, weighing 4,143.5 kilogrammes, concealed alongside three vehicles in a container at the Apapa Port. The contraband was discovered during a joint examination conducted by officers of the Nigeria

item.title: Tour de France crash and concussion cases expose limits of roadside checks
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Three of the eight riders who have abandoned the Tour de France in the opening week have suffered concussions.

item.title: World Cup final ball, halftime show outfits to be auctioned
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: A 2022 Argentina jersey signed by star player Lionel Messi is being auctioned too, as well as a US jersey signed by the United States' men's team. The post World Cup final ball, halftime show outfits to be auctioned appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Gov Eno Approves ₦1.3bn Bursary, Raises Students’ Award To ₦50,000
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Akwa Ibom State Governor, Umo Eno, has approved the release of ₦1.3 billion for the payment of bursaries to eligible students in the state, with the bursary award increased from ₦20,000 to ₦50,000 per beneficiary. The approval, announced on Tuesday, is part of the state government’s efforts to

item.title: NYSC to blacklist employers denying corps members accommodation, transportation
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The Director General of the National Youth Service Corps, Brigadier General Olakunle Nafiu, has threatened to blacklist employers who fail to provide accommodation and transportation for corps members posted to serve with them. The post NYSC to blacklist employers denying corps members accommodation

item.title: PVC registration: INEC unveils online application process
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Independent National Electoral Commission, INEC, has unveiled a new online self-service portal that allows eligible Nigerians to register for a Permanent Voter Card, PVC, transfer voting locations, update personal information and replace lost or damaged voter cards. The Commission disclosed this

item.title: Temporary Work Visa Holders Shouldn’t Stay In UK Forever, Badenoch Tells Labour
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Leader of the UK Conservative Party, Kemi Badenoch, has urged the Labour government to retain its proposed 10-year qualifying period for indefinite leave to remain (ILR), insisting that temporary work visa holders should not automatically qualify for permanent settlement in Britain. Badenoch stated

item.title: Wole Soyinka at 92; An Enigma of Intellectual Acme
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: He did not write to explain Africa to the outside world; he wrote because the African reality possessed an intrinsic, undeniable depth that demanded a global space.

item.title: Colombian national killed by ICE agent during operation in Maine
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: It comes less than a week after an undocumented migrant was fatally shot in Houston by an immigration agent.

item.title: Why SpaceX and Tesla are ‘value’ stocks, according to this fund manager
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Tesla and SpaceX may be winners in the next evolution of value investing, says the president and chief investment officer of Tsai Capital, Christopher Tsai.

item.title: Transfers: Bournemouth and Aston Villa keeping tabs on Super Eagles-eligible goalkeeper
item.dc:creator: Soccernet NG
item.description: ​English Premier League clubs Bournemouth and Aston Villa are interested in Super Eagles-eligible goalkeeper Noah Atubolu, Soccernet.ng reports. ​The 24-year-old shot-stopper has developed immensely in the last few years. In fact, he is a homegrown Freiburg talent; he rose through the ranks from the

item.title: Upload case documents for September–December pending appeals, Supreme Court directs lawyers
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Supreme Court has directed all legal practitioners with cases scheduled for hearing between September and December 2026 to The post Upload case documents for September–December pending appeals, Supreme Court directs lawyers appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: JPMorgan beats profit expectations by the most in five years, as equity-markets revenue surges
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: JPMorgan’s stock falls, even after a big profit beat, record revenue and a raised outlook for net interest income.

item.title: 8 Things To Know About New NEPZA Board Boss Muhammad Hadi Mutallab
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has approved the appointment of Muhammad Hadi Mutallab as the Board Chairman of the Nigerian Export Processing Zones Authority (NEPZA). Here are key things to know about the new NEPZA board chairman: 1. Entrepreneur and Business Leader: Mutallab is the founder of Dattaku Global

item.title: LeBron James to appear in New York with NBA future unknown
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The 41-year-old NBA all-time scoring leader has said he will not return to the Los Angeles Lakers but wants to play a 24th season with another club. The post LeBron James to appear in New York with NBA future unknown appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Intervene to avert price hike – IPMAN tells FG as Dangote begins sale in dollars
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Independent Petroleum Marketers Association of Nigeria, IPMAN, has warned that the prices of petrol, diesel and other petroleum will go up as the Dangote Refinery commences the sale of refined products in dollars. IPMAN spokesperson, Chinedu Ukadike, spoke in an interview with DAILY POST on&#160

item.title: Nigerian-Born Mother Beatrice Solomon Dies By Suicide In UK, Coroner Rules
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Tragedy struck a Nigerian family in the United Kingdom after 27-year-old Nigerian-born mother, Beatrice Solomon, died by suicide, with a UK coroner ruling that she took her own life following an inquest into her death. Solomon was found unresponsive at her home on Norris Road, Stanfield, Stoke-on-Tr

item.title: Lebanon, Israel hold talks in Rome on implementing framework deal

item.description: Beirut is hoping for progress towards securing an Israeli withdrawal from southern Lebanon.

item.title: Onanuga faults Makinde’s UN probe call over Oyo abduction
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Presidency has criticised Oyo State Governor Seyi Makinde's call for a United Nations-backed investigation into the abduction and rescue of pupils and teachers in Oriire Local Government Area, describing the demand as unnecessary and politically motivated. The post Onanuga faults Makinde’s

item.title: Let’s build a children’s public internet
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: An increasing number of people seem to agree the internet is terrible for children - allegedly addictive, destructive to self-esteem, possibly a portal to predators. Over the past year, several countries have started requiring stringent age verification or outright bans for minors. At the end of Jun

item.title: These are the toughest places in America to sell a home right now
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: From Miami to Seattle, homeowners are seeing their homes sit on the market for longer before getting an offer.

item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The Nigeria Customs Service has revealed that the value of Import Duty Exemption Certificate approvals for imported goods and equipment rose to N34 trillion in 2025, with about 60 per cent of the waivers granted for military hardware procurement. item.description: … Contaminated floodwaters, blocked drainages heighten cholera, typhoid, malaria risks…Urge govts to strengthen surveillance, stock medicines before deaths riseBy Chioma Obinna The floodwaters that swept through Lagos and several parts of Nigeria in recent weeks left behind more than submerged homes

item.title: Rubio Accuses ICC of Threatening US Sovereignty, Vows American Resistance
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: United States Secretary of State Marco Rubio has accused the International Criminal Court (ICC) of undermining American sovereignty, declaring that the administration would resist what he described as attempts by foreign institutions to interfere with the country’s legal and political system.

item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: Presidential candidate of the African Action Congress, AAC, and convener of the Take It Back Movement, Omoyele Sowore, has promised free education for all Nigerians if elected in the 2027 general elections. The human rights activist made the declaration on Monday on his official X handle, amid natio

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Aare Ona Kakanfo of Yorubaland, Iba Gani Adams, has described Prof Wole Soyinka as an enigma whose life is dedicated to the service of humanity through literature, scholarship, and activism. In a tribute to Soyinka on the occasion of his 92nd birthday on Monday, Adams said the celebration was a

PalmPay Group (“PalmPay”), a multinational fintech company providing digital financial services across high-growth e

He therefore called on teachers to embrace discipline, integrity, professionalism and ethical values in raising a generation o

The regional tournament will also serve as the qualifying competition for the 2027 Africa U-20 Cup of Nations, with the top teams earning p

item.title: Forgery charge: Alleged ‘fake’ PFIPC DG Adeyemi fails to appear in court
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Adeyemi is billed to take his plea before Justice Mohammed Umar on an eight-count charge marked FHC/ABJ/CR/562/2025, which relates to forgery, fraud and impersonation. item.title: Suspected herders attack Benue community 48 hours after Otukpo killings
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Residents fled Ondo community in Benue's Otukpo Local Government Area on Tuesday following a fresh attack by suspected armed herdsmen, barely two days after a deadly assault in the same council claimed several lives. The post Suspected herders attack Benue community 48 hours after Otukpo killings ap

item.title: JUST-IN: Again Suspected Armed Herdsmen Attack Otukpo In Benue 48 Hours After 1st Attack
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Again suspected armed herdsmen have reportedly launched fresh attacks in Otukpo Local Government Area of Benue State forty eight hours after the first attacks on Akpachi and Otukpo-Nobi communities which killed ten residents. A eyewitness account said the coordinated attacks happened in Ondo village

item.title: Kehinde Ajose: What an Empty Seat Taught Me About Creating Value
item.dc:creator: BellaNaija
item.description: Earlier this year, I attended a Sunday church service where a simple instruction from an usher would later become a powerful lesson about value, purpose and impact. With a smile, the usher walked over to me and said, “Please, fill the seat.” It was a simple instruction. I moved a few steps and occup

The post NMDPRA Seals Two Pet

item.title: Troops Arrest Suspected Foreign ISIS Operative After Foiled Borno Attack
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: Troops of Operation HADIN KAI have arrested a suspected foreign terrorist believed to have links with the Islamic State (ISIS) following a failed attack by Islamic State West Africa Province (ISWAP) fighters on Cross Kauwa in Kukawa Local Government Area of Borno State. The arrest followed an intens

item.title: SLB, Liberty Energy to Sell Power and Parts to Data Centers
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: SLB, the world’s largest oil field service provider, has agreed to partner with Liberty Energy Inc. to provide modular parts and power for data centers, the latest example of energy companies positioning themselves to benefit from the artificial intelligence boom.

item.title: Fed Is Behind The Curve as Tech Economy Booms, TS Lombard Says
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The Federal Reserve should start tightening policy to constrain the artificial intelligence boom gripping the US, TS Lombard Chief Economist Freya Beamish said.

item.title: Real Madrid, Barca Liga starts delayed
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Jose Mourinho's first La Liga game in charge of Real Madrid in his second spell at the club will be away at Espanyol on August 22 after Los Blancos' original opener was pushed back on Tuesday. The post Real Madrid, Barca Liga starts delayed appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: This region wants to build Africa’s most connected fintech ecosystem
item.dc:creator: TechCabal
item.description: WAEMU, an eight-country region, wants to build a connected fintech ecosystem that bypasses the interoperability problem much of Africa faces. At Catapult Africa 2026, investors, founders, and regulators gathered to discuss what that future would look like.

item.title: Hormuz Tensions Put Commodity Supplies at Risk Far Beyond Oil
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The latest flare-up of tensions over the Strait of Hormuz is threatening fresh global supply disruptions way beyond oil and gas.

item.title: Commissioner Threatens Legal Action Over Fake Lagos Flood Video
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Lagos State Government has vowed to pursue legal action against an X user for allegedly sharing a misleading flood video and falsely presenting it as a reflection of the flood situation in the state. The Commissioner for the Environment and Water Resources, Tokunbo Wahab, disclosed this on Tuesd

item.title: Serie A: Amorim throws Chukwueze Into new role ahead of new season
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Super Eagles winger Samuel Chukwueze was given a new role during AC Milan's first training session under newly appointed manager Ruben Amorim as preparations for the new season got underway.

item.title: Nigerian Man Allegedly Jailed In Togo For Calling Woman ‘Prostitute’
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: A Nigerian man is reportedly serving a jail term in Togo for allegedly calling a woman a “prostitute” during a heated argument. An X user John said the man, who is her neighbour, had been missing since he travelled to the country for a holiday. According to him, an argument broke out between his nei

item.title: Prosecute Nafiu Bala over access to portal claim – ADC to INEC
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The African Democratic Congress, ADC, has called on the Independent National Electoral Commission, INEC, to prosecute the party’s factional National Chairman, Nafiu Bala, over claims that he uploaded candidates’ names on the Commission’s portal. ADC’s spokesman, Bolaji Abdull

item.title: Thailand Scraps Plan to End Visa-Free Entry for Indian Tourists
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Thailand will allow 30-day visa-exempted entries by visitors from India, after a proposal to scrap visa-free entry led arrivals from the South Asian nation to drop in a blow to the vital tourism industry.

item.title: China Urges Normal Hormuz Passage as Trump Reinstates Blockade
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: China called for restoring normal passage through the Strait of Hormuz as soon as possible after President Donald Trump reinstated the US blockade of Iranian ships transiting through the vital waterway.

item.title: Firm Expands Prosthetics Training To Build Africa’s Next Generation Of Specialists
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: IfeanHealth has expanded its prosthetics and orthotics training to develop the next generation of rehabilitation specialists across Africa, bringing together healthcare stakeholders and selected beneficiaries of its scholarship programme ahead of their internship placements in Lagos and Enugu. The c

item.title: Anti-financial crime summit by CWG Plc and Clari5 brings together Nigeria’s banking executives and regulators
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: More than 160 senior executives from Nigeria’s banking and financial services industry gathered recently gathered in Lagos for the Anti-Financial Crime Summit 2026, hosted by CWG Plc in partnership with Clari5 — A Perfios Company, under the theme “Strengthening Nigeria’s Anti

item.title: Accord Elders Give Adeleke Seven-Day Ultimatum To Endorse Gbenga Hashim, Threaten To Withdraw Support
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Leaders and stakeholders of the Accord Party in Osun State have given Governor Ademola Adeleke a seven-day ultimatum to publicly declare support for the party’s presidential candidate, Dr. Gbenga Olawepo-Hashim, or risk losing their political backing ahead of the August 2026 governorshi

item.title: WAFU B U-20 Championship: Flying Eagles must fight to secure AFCON ticket – Maikaba
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Flying Eagles head coach Abdu Maikaba has said his side must work hard to excel at the WAFU B U-20 Championship, DAILY POST reports. The sub-regional tournament will serve as  qualifier for the 2027 Africa U-20 Cup of Nations. Hosts Cote d’Ivoire, Ghana, Burkina Faso, Benin Republic,

item.title: Why South African banks still charge for instant payments
item.dc:creator: TechCabal
item.description: Jacobs said customers should not have to think twice about moving their own money because of transaction fees. The debate extends beyond GoTyme's pricing strategy.

item.title: Businesses remain optimistic despite high taxes, insecurity – CBN report
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The June 2026 BES was conducted between 8 and 12 June, covering 1,900 business enterprises across Nigeria. The post Businesses remain optimistic despite high taxes, insecurity – CBN report appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Toke Makinwa’s Peach & Bronze Look Is the Wedding Guest Inspiration You Need
item.dc:creator: BellaNaija
item.description: Media personality Toke Makinwa is serving style inspiration in a structured peach and bronze traditional aso ebi gown styled with a metallic pleated gele. item.description: Academic Staff Union of Secondary Schools (ASUSS) has applauded the collaborative approach between the federal and Oyo State governments that led to the rescue of the pupils and teachers abducted in Oriire local government area of Oyo State, A statement signed by the national president of ASUSS, Com

item.title: Slain Oyo teacher’s family writes Tinubu, seeks recovery of body
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The family of Michael Oyedokun, one of the two teachers killed during the abduction of pupils and teachers in Oriire Local Government Area of Oyo State, has appealed to President Bola Tinubu to help The post Slain Oyo teacher’s family writes Tinubu, seeks recovery of body appeared first on Tribune O
